message:
test(fuzz): add SymbolRequest CBOR decode + validate + verify fuzz target (br-4p4ti)

The control-plane SymbolRequest is the fcp-protocol parser that the mesh
runs before committing any response symbols. Existing fuzz targets cover
FCPS framing, oauth, raptorq, websocket, SSE, host invoke dispatch, and
the post-verify symbol-ack path — but nothing exercises the SymbolRequest
CBOR decode + validate_hint_bounds / validate_bounds / transcript_bytes
/ verify pipeline.

A panic anywhere in that chain is a remote DoS, since the request travels
across FCPC and the receiver has no opportunity to reject it earlier than
these structural validators.

The new fuzz_symbol_request_validate target:
  - caps input at 64KiB to keep transcript building bounded
  - decodes arbitrary bytes as SymbolRequest via ciborium
  - on decode-success, drives validate_hint_bounds, validate_bounds(true),
    validate_bounds(false), has_proof_of_need, transcript_bytes, and
    verify with a deterministic Ed25519 key
  - asserts only panic-freedom — anti-amplification caps
    MAX_SYMBOLS_HARD_CAP (2001) and MAX_MISSING_HINT_ENTRIES (100) must
    be enforced by the validators, not by the decoder

Cargo.toml binary entry was added by ad125bf0; this commit lands the
fuzz target source.

Verified via cargo +nightly fuzz check fuzz_symbol_request_validate
(release profile, optimized + debuginfo, finished in ~1m 36s).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
